intent: We are building a Mac Consensus Dev Agent that will use consensus AI to develop high quality software. Use all the documents in the local repo to determine the architecture.
scope_statement:
Build the complete Crafted Dev Agent — a native macOS two-process AI coding agent consisting of a Swift/SwiftUI shell (UI, Touch ID auth, Keychain, XPC channel, process management) and a Python 3.12 backend (consensus engine with Claude+GPT-4o parallel generation, build pipeline with confidence-gated scope through operator-gated PR merge, GitHub integration, document store with FAISS vector retrieval, multi-agent coordination ledger, holistic code review, TRD development workflow with FOUNDER/ENGINEER/CONSULTANT modes, self-healing observability loop, cross-run learning via BuildMemory and BuildRules, and Mac CI runner infrastructure). The system is fully specified across 16 TRDs plus supporting documents, with five identified gaps (TRD-18 Crafted Connect SDK, deployment pipeline, Figma MCP integration, EmailNotifier contract, and WebhookReceiver details) to be resolved during PRD decomposition as the operator has directed.

Clarifications:
  - resolve as part of the PRD process with best practices
  - resolve as part of the PRD process with best practices
  - resolve as part of the PRD process with best practices
  - resolve as part of the PRD process with best practices
  - resolve as part of the PRD process with best practices

Operator on 'The 16 TRDs specify a large system — should the PRD decompos':
build the PRDs in the order that allows for maximum code efficiency

Operator on 'TRD-17 (Self-Healing) references runtime telemetry and healt':
included in the build

Operator on 'TRD-7 v2.0 CONSULTANT mode (§21) includes client summary .do':
included in the build

Operator on 'TRD-8 v3.0 Health Dashboard and Figma pipeline UI are signif':
include them in the build

prd_plan.title: Platform Foundations, Canonical Contracts, and Gap Resolution
Security Architecture and Safety Enforcement Layer
Backend Runtime, Startup Handshake, XPC Server, and Command Routing
Recovery, Thread State, Checkpointing, and Cross-Run Learning
Document Store, Parsing, Embeddings, Retrieval, and Context Safety
GitHub Integration Core, Webhooks, CI Polling, and Repository Bootstrap
Consensus Engine and Provider Orchestration
Build Pipeline Orchestrator, Scope Gate, PR Planning, and Execution Routing
Code Quality Engine: Self-Correction, Lint Gate, and Fix Loop
Multi-Agent Coordination Ledger and Collaboration Surfaces
Holistic Code Review Workflow and Fix PR Automation
TRD Development Workflow with FOUNDER, ENGINEER, and CONSULTANT Modes
Swift Shell Core: App Structure, Auth, Keychain, and Process Management
Swift-Python XPC Bridge and Shell-Backend Event Integration
SwiftUI Product UX: Build Stream, TRD UX, Health Dashboard, and Remediation Views
Document Import, Sketch Input, Figma Integration, and Figma MCP Pipeline
Crafted Connect SDK, Target Registry, Runtime Telemetry, and In-App Health Reporting
Self-Healing Observability, Detection, Diagnosis, Remediation, and Learning Loop
Notifications, EmailNotifier, Operator Alerts, and Native macOS Attention Surfaces
Mac CI Runner, Swift Validation Pipeline, Signing, Notarization, and Release Deployment
Comprehensive Testing, Regression Enforcement, and Release Validation

1.title: Repository layout, ownership map, and foundational directory structure
1.summary: Establishes the canonical source-of-truth directory structure for the entire Forge Agent monorepo. Defines top-level folders (swift-shell/, python-backend/, contracts/, docs/, schemas/, tests/, .github/), CODEOWNERS file mapping each path to its owning team/role, CONTRIBUTING.md with PR labeling conventions, and a README.md explaining the repo topology. This is the structural skeleton every subsequent PR targets.

2.title: Cross-language naming conventions and serialization standards
2.summary: Establishes the canonical source-of-truth directory structure for the entire Forge Agent monorepo. Defines top-level folders (swift-shell/, python-backend/, contracts/, docs/, schemas/, tests/, .github/), CODEOWNERS file mapping each path to its owning team/role, CONTRIBUTING.md with PR labeling conventions, and a README.md explaining the repo topology. This is the structural skeleton every subsequent PR targets.

3.title: Cross-TRD reconciliation decision log
3.summary: Establishes the canonical source-of-truth directory structure for the entire Forge Agent monorepo. Defines top-level folders (swift-shell/, python-backend/, contracts/, docs/, schemas/, tests/, .github/), CODEOWNERS file mapping each path to its owning team/role, CONTRIBUTING.md with PR labeling conventions, and a README.md explaining the repo topology. This is the structural skeleton every subsequent PR targets.

4.title: Semantic versioning, compatibility contracts, and operational acceptance limits
4.summary: Creates the Architectural Decision Record (ADR) log capturing every cross-TRD conflict resolution. Includes ADR-001 (XPC envelope: TRD-1 §6.4 vs TRD-4 §15 — resolved to single canonical envelope), ADR-002 (rate limit exemption during handshake), ADR-003 (field optionality reconciliation across TRD-10 §13 and TRD-12 §4), ADR-004 (semantic versioning for schema evolution), ADR-005 (fail-closed defaults everywhere), ADR-006 (cross-language naming mapping), ADR-007 (unknown message handling: strip-and-log), ADR-008 (retry backoff policy), ADR-009 (size limit policy). Each ADR follows Status/Context/Decision/Consequences template. Also includes the cross-TRD reconciliation matrix and resolved open questions.

5.title: Gap resolution documents for TRD-18, deployment pipeline, Figma MCP, EmailNotifier, and WebhookReceiver
5.summary: Creates the Architectural Decision Record (ADR) log capturing every cross-TRD conflict resolution. Includes ADR-001 (XPC envelope: TRD-1 §6.4 vs TRD-4 §15 — resolved to single canonical envelope), ADR-002 (rate limit exemption during handshake), ADR-003 (field optionality reconciliation across TRD-10 §13 and TRD-12 §4), ADR-004 (semantic versioning for schema evolution), ADR-005 (fail-closed defaults everywhere), ADR-006 (cross-language naming mapping), ADR-007 (unknown message handling: strip-and-log), ADR-008 (retry backoff policy), ADR-009 (size limit policy). Each ADR follows Status/Context/Decision/Consequences template. Also includes the cross-TRD reconciliation matrix and resolved open questions.

6.title: Canonical JSON schemas for XPC message types
6.summary: Defines the canonical naming convention mapping between Swift (camelCase properties, PascalCase types), Python (snake_case), and JSON wire protocol (camelCase). Specifies serialization rules: all timestamps as ISO-8601 with timezone, UUIDs as lowercase hyphenated strings, enums as SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE strings on the wire, optional fields omitted (not null) unless explicitly nullable. Includes a complete lookup table for every domain term showing its Swift name, Python name, and JSON key name. Also publishes versioning and compatibility guarantees for all shared contracts.

7.title: Canonical JSON schemas for domain models
7.summary: Adds the JSON Schema meta-schema configuration (draft 2020-12), a schema validation script (Python) that validates all .schema.json files against meta-schema, checks cross-references and $ref resolution, and a CI workflow that runs validation on every PR touching schemas/. Also defines the common schema-meta envelope, identifiers, and error shapes that all subsequent schemas reference. This tooling ensures all schemas frozen in subsequent PRs are structurally valid and internally consistent.

8.title: Canonical JSON schemas for API contracts and event types
8.summary: Adds the JSON Schema meta-schema configuration (draft 2020-12), a schema validation script (Python) that validates all .schema.json files against meta-schema, checks cross-references and $ref resolution, and a CI workflow that runs validation on every PR touching schemas/. Also defines the common schema-meta envelope, identifiers, and error shapes that all subsequent schemas reference. This tooling ensures all schemas frozen in subsequent PRs are structurally valid and internally consistent.

9.title: Schema validation test harness and domain schema tests
9.summary: Adds validation tests ensuring all PRD-001 contract directories, required documentation artifacts, schema registry files, and CODEOWNERS entries exist and follow the canonical layout established in PRs 1-4.

10.title: XPC and API schema validation tests with security-critical rejection tests
10.summary: Defines the frozen canonical JSON Schemas for the XPC message envelope (header with message_id, timestamp, schema_version, message_type, correlation_id; body as polymorphic payload; optional error block) and the XPC handshake protocol (hello, hello_ack, ready, ready_ack). Resolves conflicts between TRD-1 §6.4, TRD-4 §15, and TRD-12 §4 per ADR-001. Includes envelope versioning (schema_version field with semver), unknown field handling (strip-and-log per ADR-007), and max message size (16 MB per ADR-009).

11.title: Python canonical domain models — core types, enums, and base mixins
11.summary: Publishes canonical JSON Schemas for XPC lifecycle messages beyond handshake: heartbeat, shutdown, auth_gate, and process_control (start/stop/restart). These define the full process bootstrap and teardown contract between Swift shell and Python backend.

12.title: Python canonical domain models — PRD, PRSpec, and ThreadState
12.summary: Defines JSON Schemas for all XPC command/response message types: generate_code_request/response, review_code_request/response, build_request/response, consensus_request/response, health_check_request/response, log_stream_subscribe/event, and error_response. Each schema references the canonical envelope from PR-6. Includes discriminator field (message_type) mapping and exhaustive enum of all valid message types.

13.title: Python canonical domain models — BuildMemory, BuildRules, and learning types
13.summary: Defines JSON Schemas for all XPC command/response message types: generate_code_request/response, review_code_request/response, build_request/response, consensus_request/response, health_check_request/response, log_stream_subscribe/event, and error_response. Each schema references the canonical envelope from PR-6. Includes discriminator field (message_type) mapping and exhaustive enum of all valid message types.

14.title: Python canonical domain models — HealthIssue, ReviewIssue, and TelemetryEvent
14.summary: Defines frozen JSON Schemas for the core workflow domain models. PRD: id, title, status enum, summary, sections array, acceptance_criteria, linked_trd_ids, created_at, updated_at. PRSpec: id, prd_id, title, impl_files, test_files, depends_on, estimated_complexity enum, security_critical flag. ThreadState: thread_id, mode enum (FOUNDER/ENGINEER/CONSULTANT), current_prd_id, current_prspec_id, conversation_history reference, active_tool_calls, created_at, last_active_at. TargetRegistry: target_id, name, platform, build_system enum, dependencies array, health_status enum, last_build_id.

15.title: Python canonical domain models — TargetRegistry and XPC message models
15.summary: Defines frozen JSON Schemas for cross-run learning and build management models. BuildMemory: memory_id, build_id, outcome enum (SUCCESS/FAILURE/PARTIAL), error_signatures array, fix_patterns array, duration_ms, timestamp. BuildRules: rule_id, trigger_condition (JSONPath expression), action enum (SKIP/RETRY/ESCALATE/APPLY_FIX), confidence_threshold float, source enum (LEARNED/MANUAL), created_from_build_id. Also includes memory provenance and applicability window schemas.

16.title: Python forge-models package finalization — exports, py.typed, and metadata
16.summary: Defines frozen JSON Schemas for observability and review domain models. HealthIssue: issue_id, component, severity enum (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), description, detected_at, resolved_at, auto_remediation_attempted bool, remediation_action. ReviewIssue: issue_id, file_path, line_range, severity enum, category enum (SECURITY/PERFORMANCE/STYLE/BUG/DESIGN), description, suggestion, confidence float. TelemetryEvent: event_id, event_type enum, source_component, timestamp, duration_ms, metadata dict, trace_id, span_id (OpenTelemetry compatible). Includes observability common types.

17.title: Swift canonical domain models — core types, enums, and protocols
17.summary: Adds canonical message contracts for task dispatch, consensus generation, build execution, code review findings, operator gating, PR proposal, merge intent, and status reporting flows. These workflow messages reference domain models from PRs 9-11.

18.title: Swift canonical domain models — XPC message types and envelope
18.summary: Adds canonical message contracts for task dispatch, consensus generation, build execution, code review findings, operator gating, PR proposal, merge intent, and status reporting flows. These workflow messages reference domain models from PRs 9-11.

19.title: Swift canonical domain models — HealthIssue, ReviewIssue, TelemetryEvent, and TargetRegistry
19.summary: Publishes canonical contracts for document ingestion, vector retrieval requests/results, multi-agent coordination ledger events, assignment claims, lock semantics, and reconciliation messages.

20.title: Swift ForgeModels package finalization — public API surface and module exports
20.summary: Comprehensive test suite that loads every schema in schemas/xpc/, validates it against JSON Schema draft 2020-12 meta-schema, verifies all $ref pointers resolve to existing schemas, checks that the message-type-enum exhaustively covers all message schemas, validates example payloads (one valid + one invalid per schema), and ensures envelope.schema.json is referenced by every command/response schema.

21.title: Cross-language serialization round-trip tests with golden fixtures
21.summary: Test suite that validates every schema in schemas/domain/ against meta-schema, checks $ref resolution, validates example payloads for each domain model (PRD, PRSpec, ThreadState, BuildMemory, BuildRules, TargetRegistry, HealthIssue, ReviewIssue, TelemetryEvent), verifies enum consistency across schemas (e.g., severity enums match between HealthIssue and ReviewIssue), and confirms naming conventions compliance (all JSON keys are camelCase per NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md).

22.title: Acceptance contract behavioral tests — unknown messages, size limits, retry, fail-closed
22.summary: Test suite covering the full set of workflow, retrieval, and coordination event schemas from PRs 12-13, including $ref resolution against domain schemas, required-field conformance, and example payload validation.

23.title: PRD-001 completion checklist, contract freeze certification, and final REPO_LAYOUT update
23.summary: Defines the canonical behavioral contracts as structured specification documents with machine-readable contract definitions. Covers: rate limits (100 msg/sec XPC steady-state, handshake exempt per ADR-002, 50 emails/hr, 100 req/min SDK), size limits (16 MB XPC message, 1 MB webhook payload, 512 KB single file in code generation), retry policy (exponential backoff: base 1s, factor 2, max 5 retries, jitter ±20%), circuit breaker (open after 5 consecutive failures, half-open probe after 30s), fail-closed defaults (all security boundaries reject on ambiguity), timeout contracts (XPC handshake 10s, code generation 120s, build 300s, health check 5s), unknown message handling policy, and dead-letter behavior.

24.title: Behavioral acceptance contracts: rate limits, size limits, retry, and fail-closed (part 2/2)
24.summary: Defines the canonical behavioral contracts as structured specification documents with machine-readable contract definitions. Covers: rate limits (100 msg/sec XPC steady-state, handshake exempt per ADR-002, 50 emails/hr, 100 req/min SDK), size limits (16 MB XPC message, 1 MB webhook payload, 512 KB single file in code generation), retry policy (exponential backoff: base 1s, factor 2, max 5 retries, jitter ±20%), circuit breaker (open after 5 consecutive failures, half-open probe after 30s), fail-closed defaults (all security boundaries reject on ambiguity), timeout contracts (XPC handshake 10s, code generation 120s, build 300s, health check 5s), unknown message handling policy, and dead-letter behavior.

25.title: Semantic versioning, schema compatibility contracts, and version lock
25.summary: Defines the schema evolution and compatibility contract per ADR-004. Specifies: semver rules for schemas (patch = documentation-only, minor = additive fields with defaults, major = breaking changes), compatibility matrix (consumers must accept unknown fields from newer minor versions), schema changelog format, deprecation policy (minimum 2 minor versions before removal), and the schema_version negotiation protocol in XPC handshake. Includes a CHANGELOG.md template and version lock file.

26.title: Tests: validate behavioral contracts and cross-contract consistency
26.summary: Test suite that validates machine-readable behavioral contracts are internally consistent and structurally correct. Validates: rate-limits.json values are positive integers, retry-policy.json backoff factor produces values within timeout-contracts.json limits, circuit-breaker.json half-open interval > 0, all timeout values referenced in XPC schemas exist in timeout-contracts.json, and size-limits.json values are consistent with XPC envelope maxLength constraints.

27.title: Gap resolution: TRD-18 Crafted Connect SDK specification
27.summary: Resolves Gap #1 by authoring the missing TRD-18 specification for Crafted Connect SDK. Defines the SDK's purpose (external tool integration layer), public API surface (register_tool, invoke_tool, list_tools, health_check), authentication model (API key with HMAC signatures), transport (HTTP/2 with JSON payloads conforming to canonical serialization standards), rate limiting (100 req/min per tool), error taxonomy, capability negotiation, and versioning contract. Follows best practices: idempotent operations, exponential backoff, circuit breaker pattern.

28.title: Gap resolution: Deployment pipeline specification (TRD-7 → TRD-3)
28.summary: Resolves Gap #2 by specifying the deployment pipeline that connects TRD-7 (PR workflow) to TRD-3 (build pipeline). Defines pipeline stages (PR merge trigger → build validation → confidence gate check → staging deploy → canary validation → production deploy), artifact format (Docker images tagged with git SHA + semver), rollback contract (automatic on health check failure within 5min window), environment promotion rules, release gating, and operator-gated merge approval interface.

29.title: Gap resolution: Figma MCP integration specification
29.summary: Resolves Gap #3 by specifying the Figma MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration referenced in TRD-8 §14. Defines the MCP server interface for Figma (read_component, list_design_tokens, export_assets, get_component_hierarchy), authentication (Figma personal access token stored in Keychain, accessed via XPC), caching strategy (5-minute TTL for component metadata, 1-hour for exported assets), error handling for Figma API rate limits (429 with Retry-After), and the data flow from Figma → MCP server → Health Dashboard UI.

30.title: Gap resolution: EmailNotifier contract specification
30.summary: Resolves Gap #4 by defining the notification interface (send_notification with recipient, subject, template_id, context dict), transport (SMTP with TLS or SendGrid API), retry policy (3 attempts, exponential backoff), rate limit (50 emails/hour), template registry, PII handling (no PII in logs, redacted in telemetry), delivery guarantee, and failure behavior.

31.title: Gap resolution: WebhookReceiver specification
31.summary: Resolves Gap #5 by defining the inbound webhook interface (receive_event with HMAC-SHA256 signature verification), supported event types (github.push, github.pr, github.review, ci.complete), request validation, replay protection, idempotency (event_id deduplication with 24h window), acknowledgement semantics, redelivery handling, and the mapping from webhook events to internal domain events.

32.title: Tests: validate gap resolution schemas and security invariants
32.summary: Test suite for the five gap resolution schemas (Crafted Connect SDK, deployment pipeline, Figma MCP, EmailNotifier, WebhookReceiver). Validates each schema against meta-schema, tests valid and invalid fixtures, verifies HMAC signature fields are present in security-critical schemas (webhook, SDK), confirms enum values are consistent with main domain model enums, and checks that all $ref pointers from gap schemas into main schemas resolve correctly.

33.title: Canonical example payloads for cross-language payload mapping
33.summary: Adds normative example payloads demonstrating Swift-to-JSON and Python-to-JSON field mappings, optional-field encoding, version negotiation, and representative domain/message instances for every schema category (XPC, domain, workflow, gaps).

34.title: Tests: validate canonical examples against published schemas
34.summary: Validates all normative example payloads against the canonical schemas and compatibility policies so downstream implementation teams can trust them as executable references.

35.title: Python canonical model definitions (Pydantic v2 BaseModel) (part 1/3)
35.summary: Implements Python 3.12 canonical data models as Pydantic v2 BaseModel classes that are the single source of truth for the Python backend, auto-validated against the frozen JSON schemas. Includes all domain models (PRD, PRSpec, ThreadState, BuildMemory, BuildRules, TargetRegistry, HealthIssue, ReviewIssue, TelemetryEvent) and all XPC message types (envelope, handshake, commands, responses). Each model includes to_wire()/from_wire() methods handling snake_case↔camelCase conversion per NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md. Includes JSON schema export validation ensuring Pydantic models match frozen schemas.

36.title: Python canonical model definitions (Pydantic v2 BaseModel) (part 2/3)
36.summary: Implements Python 3.12 canonical data models as Pydantic v2 BaseModel classes that are the single source of truth for the Python backend, auto-validated against the frozen JSON schemas. Includes all domain models (PRD, PRSpec, ThreadState, BuildMemory, BuildRules, TargetRegistry, HealthIssue, ReviewIssue, TelemetryEvent) and all XPC message types (envelope, handshake, commands, responses). Each model includes to_wire()/from_wire() methods handling snake_case↔camelCase conversion per NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md. Includes JSON schema export validation ensuring Pydantic models match frozen schemas.

37.title: Python canonical model definitions (Pydantic v2 BaseModel) (part 3/3)
37.summary: Implements Python 3.12 canonical data models as Pydantic v2 BaseModel classes that are the single source of truth for the Python backend, auto-validated against the frozen JSON schemas. Includes all domain models (PRD, PRSpec, ThreadState, BuildMemory, BuildRules, TargetRegistry, HealthIssue, ReviewIssue, TelemetryEvent) and all XPC message types (envelope, handshake, commands, responses). Each model includes to_wire()/from_wire() methods handling snake_case↔camelCase conversion per NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md. Includes JSON schema export validation ensuring Pydantic models match frozen schemas.

38.title: Swift canonical model definitions (Codable structs) (part 1/2)
38.summary: Implements Swift canonical data models as Codable structs/enums that are the single source of truth for the Swift shell. Includes all domain models and XPC message types matching the frozen JSON schemas. Each model uses CodingKeys for camelCase wire format mapping. Includes JSONEncoder/JSONDecoder configuration (ISO-8601 dates, sorted keys for deterministic output). Provides a SchemaValidator utility that validates Swift model JSON output against the frozen schemas at test time.

39.title: Swift canonical model definitions (Codable structs) (part 2/2)
39.summary: Implements Swift canonical data models as Codable structs/enums that are the single source of truth for the Swift shell. Includes all domain models and XPC message types matching the frozen JSON schemas. Each model uses CodingKeys for camelCase wire format mapping. Includes JSONEncoder/JSONDecoder configuration (ISO-8601 dates, sorted keys for deterministic output). Provides a SchemaValidator utility that validates Swift model JSON output against the frozen schemas at test time.

40.title: Cross-language round-trip conformance tests
40.summary: Integration test suite that verifies Python and Swift models produce byte-identical JSON for the same logical payload (after key sorting). For each domain model and XPC message type: generates a canonical fixture JSON, deserializes it in both Python and Swift, re-serializes, and compares output. Catches any naming convention drift, enum value mismatch, or serialization divergence between the two implementations.

41.title: CI pipeline for schema, contract, and conformance validation on all PRs
41.summary: GitHub Actions workflows that run on every PR: validates all JSON schemas, runs all contract consistency tests, runs cross-language conformance tests (when both model packages are present), and gates merge on zero schema violations. Includes caching for Python/Swift dependencies, matrix strategy for schema validation vs model tests, and status check requirements configuration.

42.title: PRD-001 acceptance checklist and downstream adoption contract
42.summary: Defines the final acceptance checklist for PRD-001 and the explicit rule that downstream PRDs and implementation PRs must import these contracts without divergence, including change-control requirements and the process for requesting contract amendments.
